metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2129
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Co-localizes with acetylcholine receptors (AChRs) in mouse NMJ, fluorescence imaging + fig4. Rabsyn is surface expressed, can be deleted/rescued by mutation of AChR interacting domain in myoblasts.

"Altogether, these results strongly suggest that AChRs serve as a mediator for the targeting and association of rapsyn with the postsynaptic membrane at synaptic sites.". Experimental description: PMID:11087759 from Jonathan Cohen's group appears to be the source for the Rapsyn-EGFP constructs. This paper mentions the construct is based on mouse species.

the cultures were also based on mouse species;
"MICE, MUSCLE DENERVATION, PLASMID ELECTROPORATION, ACHR LABELING, AND NMJ IMAGING. In this study, female non-Swiss albino mice (6-10 weeks old, 20-30 g) were used."

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
